LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP AND PAULA WEBER'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. THIS IS NOT ONE OF THOSE 'RAREST OF CASES' WHERE DUE PROCESS REQUIRES DISMISSAL. WHILE SOME OF DEFENDANTS' CAUSATION DEFENSE THEORIES (MOST NOTABLY THE THEORY THAT PLAINTIFF RELIED ON ADVICE OF ITS OWN COUNSEL, NOT ON DEFENDANTS' ADVICE) ARE LIKELY MADE HARDER TO DEVELOP AND/OR ESTABLISH DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S INVOCATION OF PRIVILEGE, THAT IS AN INSUFFICIENT BASIS TO REQUIRE DISMISSAL. PLAINTIFF'S INVOCATION OF THE PRIVILEGE DOES NOT IMPEDE DEFENDANTS FROM PURSUING A VARIETY OF POSSIBLE DEFENSES INCLUDING CAUSATION DEFENSE THEORIES (MOST NOTABLY DEFENDANTS DID NOT PROVIDE ADVICE THAT COULD REASONABLY HAVE BEEN RELIED ON) AND THUS THE DRASTIC REMEDY OF DISMISSAL IS INAPPROPRIATE, PARTICULARLY SINCE DEFENDANTS ARE UNIMPEDED IN THEIR USE OF WHAT THEY SAID OR WAS SAID TO THEM. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS ARE INVITED BY COURT TO FILE A MOTION TO RECONSIDER JUDGE KAHN'S ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S DISCOVERY MOTION HEARD BY HIM WHICH WAS FILED ON OCTOBER 26, 2011. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077 =302/HEK

Joel Kozberg, Esq., of Kozberg & Bodell LLP, Tel. 310.553.1333, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, LLP and Paula Weber's Motion To Re-Open Depositions - The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles as follows: The Motion to Re-Open Depositions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff placed the communications of Mr. Wilkinson related to the guaranteed compensation program at issue and therefore impliedly waived the attorney-client privilege with regard to those communications. The depositions of the following witnesses are re-opened to permit questioning regarding these communications: Wilkinson, Liu, Cherng, Davin, Brandt and Cheung. No waiver under Evidence Code 958. The discovery cut-off date is now March 23, 2012 solely for the purpose of permitting these depositions to occur and not for any other discovery purpose. The Court invites the Plaintiff to move for reconsideration of the prior ruling of Judge Giorgi with regard to documents related to these same communications. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Mtn To Dismiss; Notice Of Lodgment
Off Calendar Dec-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOEL M. KOZBERG, TEL. NO. (310) 553-1333 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. PAMELA PHILLIPS & ATTY. SEAN M. SELEGUE OF HOWARD RICE, TEL. NO. 677-6333 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP AND PAULA WEBER'S MOTION To Dismiss IS Denied. This is not one of those 'rarest of cases' where due process requires dismissal. While some of defendants' causation defense theories (most notably the theory that plaintiff relied on advice of its own counsel, not on defendants' advice) are likely made harder to develop and/or establish due to plaintiff's invocation of privilege, that is an insufficient basis to require dismissal. Plaintiff's invocation of the privilege does not impede defendants from pursuing a variety of possible defenses including causation defense theories (most notably defendants did not provide advice that could reasonably have been relied on) and thus the drastic remedy of dismissal is inappropriate, particularly since defendants are unimpeded in their use of what they said or was said to them. Counsel for defendants are invited by court to file a motion to reconsider Judge Kahn's order denying defendant's discovery motion heard by him which was filed on October 26, 2011. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
